content.speakerText: You led a student volunteer group—what made you decide to step up, and how'd you keep everyone motivated?
content.speakerTextCn: 你领导过学生志愿团队，是什么让你决定担起责任？怎么让大家保持动力？
expectedAnswers: I saw others were stressed, so I took charge and made a task rotation. We also had a weekly snack time—it made people feel appreciated.
Someone needed to organize it, and I enjoy planning. I reminded the group of our mission and thanked them regularly.
I stepped up because no one else would. I kept morale high by recognizing effort, not just results, and we had pizza every Friday.
evaluationCriteria: 是否清晰说明了‘为什么接手’和‘如何激励’两个关键点
是否使用了具体策略（如轮值、庆祝、食物奖励）而非空泛描述
是否体现了人性化细节和团队关怀，语言自然流畅

content.speakerText: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your team. How'd you handle it?
content.speakerTextCn: 说说你和团队有分歧的时候，你是怎么处理的？
expectedAnswers: We had different views on the deadline—I shared data showing the original plan was too tight, then suggested breaking it into phases. They liked the idea.
I didn't agree at first, but instead of pushing back, I asked, 'Can we test my version for a week?' It turned out to be more efficient.
I voiced my concern calmly, listened to their reasons, and we compromised by combining both approaches. The final result was better than either original plan.
evaluationCriteria: 是否避免了对抗性语言（如‘you’re wrong’），展现了建设性沟通
是否包含具体行动（如展示数据、提议测试、寻求妥协）
是否体现了团队导向而非个人胜利，语言自然有逻辑

content.speakerText: Describe a time you helped a teammate grow or improve.
content.speakerTextCn: 描述一次你帮助团队成员成长或进步的经历。
expectedAnswers: One member was shy in meetings, so I invited them to lead a small part of the presentation. I gave feedback afterward—they got much more confident.
I noticed a teammate struggled with time management, so I shared my planner template and we checked in weekly. Their output improved by 40%.
I paired them with a more experienced member for mentorship and encouraged them to speak up. They later led their own project successfully.
evaluationCriteria: 是否明确说明了对方的挑战和你的具体帮助方式
是��体现了支持性而非控制性领导风格
是否包含结果或成长的证据，语言真实、有细节
